text:
SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT

Department of Pathology and Laboratory Medicine
Genitourinary and Soft Tissue Pathology Section

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
PATIENT: Margaret Okonkwo
MRN: [REDACTED]
DATE OF PROCEDURE: [DATE ADDED PROGRAMMATICALLY]
DATE RECEIVED: [DATE ADDED PROGRAMMATICALLY]
DATE REPORTED: [DATE ADDED PROGRAMMATICALLY]
ATTENDING PATHOLOGIST: [REDACTED]
CLINICIAN: [REDACTED], MD вЂ” Genitourinary Medical Oncology
CLINICAL HISTORY: 63-year-old female with known Stage IV clear cell renal cell carcinoma (ccRCC), primary right kidney (11.8 cm, pT3bN2, WHO-ISUP grade 4, sarcomatoid differentiation 22%), status post cytoreductive right radical nephrectomy with retroperitoneal lymph node dissection and IVC thrombectomy. Currently on nivolumab maintenance therapy (first-line ipilimumab/nivolumab initiated approximately 8 weeks post-nephrectomy). Recent restaging CT identified a new 1.3 cm right adrenal nodule (not present on baseline post-operative imaging) and mixed response with progression of right lower lobe pulmonary lesion. CT-guided biopsy of right adrenal nodule submitted for histologic evaluation to confirm or exclude metastatic disease prior to planning further local therapy.
ACCESSION NUMBER: SP-[REDACTED]
SPECIMEN DESIGNATION: A
в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ

SPECIMEN SUBMITTED:
A: Right adrenal nodule, CT-guided core needle biopsy (18-gauge, 3 cores submitted)

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:

A. RIGHT ADRENAL NODULE, CT-GUIDED CORE NEEDLE BIOPSY:
   вЂ” METASTATIC RENAL CELL CARCINOMA, CLEAR CELL TYPE
   вЂ” See comment.

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ

COMMENT:
The morphologic and immunohistochemical profile is consistent with metastatic renal cell carcinoma, clear cell type, in the clinical context of the patient's known right-sided primary. Three cores of viable tumor are present. The tumor cells are arranged in compact nests and alveolar-type structures separated by a delicate arborizing sinusoidal vascular network, a hallmark of ccRCC architecture. Tumor cells display characteristic optically clear to pale eosinophilic cytoplasm due to dissolved intracytoplasmic glycogen and lipid. Nuclei are round to oval with moderate pleomorphism and occasional small nucleoli; nuclear grade corresponds to WHO-ISUP Grade 3 based on the current specimen (nucleoli visible at 10x). No sarcomatoid or rhabdoid differentiation is identified in the cores sampled; however, given the known sarcomatoid component (22%) in the nephrectomy specimen, sampling limitation cannot be excluded. Mitotic figures are present (approximately 3 per 10 high-power fields). Necrosis is focally present, comprising less than 10% of the biopsy material.

The immunohistochemical panel is confirmatory: PAX8 is strongly and diffusely nuclear positive; CA9 (carbonic anhydrase IX) demonstrates strong diffuse membranous positivity in a cup-shaped pattern typical of ccRCC; CD10 is positive (membranous); vimentin is positive; CK7 is focally and weakly positive. Adrenal cortical markers (SF-1, Melan-A, inhibin) are negative, excluding primary adrenocortical neoplasm. Chromogranin A and synaptophysin are negative, excluding adrenal medullary (pheochromocytoma) origin.

Ki-67 proliferation index: approximately 22% of tumor nuclei positive.

These findings, in aggregate with the clinical history and prior histologic diagnosis, confirm that this right adrenal nodule represents metastatic clear cell renal cell carcinoma from the known right renal primary.

PD-L1 IHC (22C3 antibody): PENDING вЂ” ordered at the request of the treating oncologist; results will be issued as an addendum.

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ

ANCILLARY STUDIES:

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY (performed on specimen A; see comment above):
  PAX8 (MRQ-50 clone): POSITIVE вЂ” strong, diffuse nuclear staining
  CA9 / Carbonic Anhydrase IX (polyclonal): POSITIVE вЂ” strong, diffuse membranous (cup-shaped pattern)
  CD10 (56C6 clone): POSITIVE вЂ” membranous
  Vimentin (V9 clone): POSITIVE вЂ” diffuse cytoplasmic
  CK7 (OV-TL12/30 clone): FOCALLY POSITIVE вЂ” weak, patchy
  CK20: NEGATIVE
  SF-1 (N1665 clone): NEGATIVE вЂ” excludes adrenocortical origin
  Melan-A (A103 clone): NEGATIVE
  Inhibin-alpha: NEGATIVE
  Chromogranin A: NEGATIVE
  Synaptophysin: NEGATIVE
  Ki-67 (MIB-1 clone): Approximately 22% nuclear labeling index
  PD-L1 22C3 (Agilent/DAKO): PENDING вЂ” addendum to follow

Note: All IHC controls performed satisfactorily.

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ

GROSS PATHOLOGY DESCRIPTION:

Specimen A is received fresh in a labeled container bearing the patient's name and medical record number, designated "Right adrenal nodule, core biopsy." The container holds three tan-white to pale yellow, cylindrical core needle biopsy fragments submitted entirely in one cassette. Fragments measure 0.9 cm, 0.8 cm, and 0.7 cm in length, each approximately 0.1 cm in diameter. Tissue is soft and slightly friable. No hemorrhage or obvious necrosis is grossly appreciated. The specimen is entirely submitted in cassette A1, following formalin fixation for approximately 6 hours prior to processing.

Cassette summary:
  A1 вЂ” All three core biopsy fragments, entirely submitted (RSS)

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:

Multiple histologic levels are examined. Three cores of adrenal tissue are identified, extensively replaced by neoplastic epithelioid cells. Residual adrenal cortical tissue is present at the periphery of one core and serves as an internal landmark. The neoplastic cells are arranged in compact nests and anastomosing trabeculae supported by a richly vascularized, thin-walled sinusoidal network. Tumor cell cytoplasm is abundant, clear to finely granular pale eosinophilic, with well-defined cell borders. Nuclei are round to ovoid, eccentrically placed in some cells, with moderate nuclear pleomorphism; nucleoli are conspicuous at 10x objective magnification (WHO-ISUP Grade 3). Occasional bi-nucleated forms are present. Mitotic figures are identified, approximately 3 per 10 HPF. No atypical mitoses are seen.

A small focus of coagulative necrosis is present in one core (estimated <10% of total submitted tissue). No lymphovascular invasion is definitively identified within the biopsy cores. The adrenal capsule is not evaluable on needle biopsy specimen.

No sarcomatoid differentiation (spindle cell morphology, stormiform architecture) is identified in the sampled material. No rhabdoid morphology is present. No heterologous elements are seen.

Fungal organisms not seen on H&E sections; GMS/PAS stains not performed (clinical indication not specified).

в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ

ATTENDING PATHOLOGIST ATTESTATION:
I have personally reviewed all slides and rendered the above diagnosis.

[Electronic signature вЂ” [REDACTED], MD, FCAP]
Board Certified Anatomic and Clinical Pathology
Genitourinary Pathology Fellowship Trained
